title: rosa communis var. andegavensis
intro: Rosa communis var. andegavensis, also known as the wild rose or European rose, is a plant species belonging to the Rosa genus and is native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rosa communis var. andegavensis include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or oil infusion for use in herbal remedies, with the petals often being dried for use in potpourri and as a natural dye.
